_purpose: A growing log of every named technique demonstrated or discovered in the story. Structured for game compatibility — each entry is concrete enough to implement as a game ability. Entries are added automatically by the pipeline after each episode.
_game_note: This file is the living skill tree of Arion World. When the game is built, this becomes the ability database. Every technique has: which branch it belongs to, what it costs, what it does, who can learn it, and when it first appeared in the story.

techniques.name: Echo Sight (Basic)
techniques.description: Passive perception of emotional and event residue left on objects and places. The practitioner feels impressions — not full visions, not complete information. Like reading a room that remembers what happened in it.
techniques.game_mechanic_concept: Passive ability: when player examines an object or enters a location, receives fragmented impressions of past events. Higher proficiency = clearer impressions, longer history accessible.
techniques.cost: No active cost. Passive. Overwhelming in high-residue environments.
techniques.how_to_learn: Most people with any Resonance awareness develop this unconsciously. Conscious control requires a teacher or significant self-directed practice.
variations_known: Can be suppressed — important for Resonants who find high-residue environments painful
Can be focused on a specific time period with training
techniques.visual_effect_tags: character eyes glowing faint amber, ghostly transparent impressions overlaid on the scene, echo residue visible as golden haze, soft amber light at vision edges

techniques.name: Thread Weaving (Basic)
techniques.description: Active manipulation of Resonance energy — shaping light, heat, minor force. The Guild's curriculum covers this and calls it mastery. It is not mastery. It is the first floor of a very tall building.
techniques.game_mechanic_concept: Active ability tree: light manipulation (illumination, blinding), heat manipulation (warming, minor ignition), force application (push/pull on small objects). Costs Resonance energy per use.
techniques.cost: Moderate Resonance energy. Sustainable for trained practitioners in short bursts.
techniques.how_to_learn: Guild curriculum — available to anyone who passes Guild entry assessment. Also self-teachable given the right starting point.
variations_known: Precision weaving — smaller effect, much lower cost, much harder (the water ball principle)
Combined weaving — two effects simultaneously, exponentially harder
techniques.visual_effect_tags: shimmering threads of resonance light being shaped by hands, glowing light strands woven between fingers, visible energy manipulation, soft white-gold luminescence

techniques.name: Precision Water Compression
techniques.description: Rather than generating large-scale water manipulation, the practitioner compresses a small volume of water to extreme density and accelerates it to high velocity. The result: a projectile the size of a coin that hits with more force than a thrown boulder. Uses a fraction of the energy of conventional water magic. Near-invisible in flight.
techniques.game_mechanic_concept: Precision ranged attack: low energy cost, high damage, minimal visual telegraph. Requires significant Resonance Arts investment to unlock but is far more efficient than equivalent-damage conventional techniques.
techniques.cost: Low Resonance energy — the efficiency is the point.
techniques.how_to_learn: Cannot be learned through Guild curriculum. Requires understanding of pressure physics and Resonance interaction with liquids at a mechanical level. Self-discovery or a very specific teacher.
techniques.why_it_matters: This technique exemplifies the core philosophy: understanding beats power. A Tier 1 practitioner who discovers this can threaten a Tier 3 practitioner who relies on brute Resonance output.
techniques.visual_effect_tags: coin-sized compressed water sphere with intense light refraction halo, near-invisible high-velocity water projectile, subtle shimmer trail in flight path, no visible energy buildup until release

techniques.name: Resonance Clone
techniques.description: Creates a physical copy of the practitioner capable of independent action. The clone shares the practitioner's knowledge at moment of creation. As the clone acts and experiences, it accumulates new experience. On dissolution, the practitioner may attempt to recover what the clone learned — this recovery is imperfect and requires training.
techniques.game_mechanic_concept: Utility ability: create a temporary duplicate for combat, exploration, or parallel tasks. Advanced versions allow partial knowledge recovery on dissolution. Clone has a health pool separate from practitioner.
techniques.cost: High initial cost. Clone maintenance is ongoing. Knowledge recovery is its own ability with its own cost.
techniques.how_to_learn: Aethon tradition primarily. Not impossible to discover independently but extremely rare. The Guild does not teach it.
variations_known: Combat clone (standard — clone fights, takes damage, dissolves)
Study clone (Aethon innovation — clone sent to learn a specific thing, optimised for knowledge recovery on dissolution)
Persistence clone (theoretical — clone that survives longer than standard by being given independent Resonance sustaining — nobody has confirmed this works)
techniques.visual_effect_tags: translucent duplicate figure with faint pale blue-white resonance outline, same appearance as original but slightly less solid, subtle chromatic fringe at clone edges, two versions of the same person in frame

techniques.name: Shamanic Resonance Reading
techniques.description: The Vorath shamanic tradition for communicating with the Shattered — people who exist partially across multiple timelines. Requires placing the practitioner's perception partially out of normal timeline phase, creating a shared perceptual space where Shattered individuals can communicate without the cognitive overload of full timeline-crossing.
techniques.game_mechanic_concept: Communication ability: allows conversation with Shattered NPCs/characters who cannot otherwise communicate coherently. Also provides brief timeline-adjacent perception — flickers of other timelines' versions of the current location.
techniques.cost: Moderate. Disorienting. Prolonged use causes temporary timeline-anchor loosening (practitioner briefly becomes partially Shattered themselves).
techniques.how_to_learn: Vorath shamanic lineage primarily. Theory teachable; practice requires a Shattered individual willing to attempt contact.
techniques.visual_effect_tags: practitioner with slight chromatic aberration aura, partial timeline desync effect, shattered individual visible as overlapping fractured translucent images, out-of-sync visual distortion around both figures, fractured purple-white light

techniques.name: Material Transmutation
techniques.description: The foundational alchemical act — declare input, mass, and desired output clearly, then channel Open Path energy to facilitate the structural conversion. The matter restructures at the particle level, releasing or absorbing energy proportional to the complexity of the conversion. Cannot create from nothing. Cannot increase total mass. Simple conversions (iron to steel) take seconds; complex conversions (coal to diamond) take sustained hours. The practitioner must understand both source and target material at a deep level — transmuting what you don't understand produces results you didn't intend.
techniques.game_mechanic_concept: Core alchemy ability: transmute one inorganic material into another of equal mass. Base ability unlocks all alchemy techniques. Complexity rating of target material determines time and Resonance energy cost. Failure on high-complexity transmutations produces unexpected substances — typically inert but occasionally hazardous.
techniques.cost: Low to moderate Resonance energy. Scales steeply with conversion complexity.
techniques.how_to_learn: Requires alchemy training — either Guild curriculum (limited) or direct practitioner instruction. Self-teaching possible with access to Founding Age texts but extremely slow.
variations_known: Accelerated transmutation (higher energy cost, faster completion)
Precision transmutation (converts only a portion of available mass with surgical accuracy)
Cascade transmutation (converts material A to B in contact with C, triggering C-to-D — chain reactions of limited scope)
techniques.visual_effect_tags: hands glowing amber-gold with alchemical light, matter visibly restructuring between practitioner's hands, particles lifting and resettling in new geometric configuration, soft chemical smoke at transformation interface, substance glowing at conversion peak, transformation geometry visible as lattice lines

techniques.name: Resonance Inscription
techniques.description: Encoding a conditional transformation instruction into an object by writing it in resonance energy — the instruction is embedded in the material structure, invisible to ordinary sight, readable by any practitioner with Resonance perception. The object performs the inscribed transformation when specified conditions are met. The inscription must be complete and internally consistent — incomplete inscriptions produce incomplete transformations, which means unpredictable transformations. The technique produces items that function without any Resonance ability in the user.
techniques.game_mechanic_concept: Crafting ability: create items with conditional alchemical effects. Specify trigger condition and transformation effect at crafting time. Item activates transformation when condition is met, usable repeatedly until inscription degrades. Complexity of inscription determines crafting time and Resonance cost. Advanced: multi-conditional inscriptions that respond to sequences rather than single triggers.
techniques.cost: High, scaling with inscription complexity and desired activation count. Inscription errors produce misconfigured items — failure mode discovered only at activation.
techniques.how_to_learn: Specialized Inscription Alchemy study. Not in standard Guild curriculum — available through advanced licensed training or direct practitioner instruction.
variations_known: Physical inscription (resonance script carved into surface — more stable, visible to Resonance perception)
Pure resonance inscription (no physical component — completely invisible, more complex to produce, less stable over time)
techniques.visual_effect_tags: glowing amber-gold script and geometric formulae being traced into object surface, inscription burning into material then fading to invisible, faint resonance shimmer persisting on inscribed surface, practitioner's finger trailing light as it writes, completed object with subtle warm inner glow, script visible briefly in resonance perception

techniques.name: Essence Distillation
techniques.description: Extracting the defining essence of a substance, experience, or quality into concentrated form. From inert materials: the active compound at many times its natural concentration. From living sources (with consent and presence): the experiential core of a specific quality — courage at the moment of demonstration, calm in the middle of crisis, the last perception of a dying person. The product is a small volume of condensed essence that can be administered to transfer something of the original quality to the recipient. Imperfect transfer — the recipient experiences 'something like' the original, not a perfect copy. Genuinely controversial. Technically legal in grey areas the Guild has never fully resolved.
techniques.game_mechanic_concept: Crafting ability: produce single-use consumable vials with specific quality effects. Material distillation produces medicine, poisons, reagents. Experiential distillation produces consumables that temporarily grant a specific quality (courage, focus, calm, heightened perception). Quality of output scales with intensity of source material/moment. Using the vial grants temporary effect for 1-4 hours depending on distillation quality.
techniques.cost: Moderate to high. Living-source distillation requires the source's cooperation and temporarily depletes the extracted quality from the source — recovering within 24-72 hours.
techniques.how_to_learn: Advanced alchemy study. Material distillation in Guild curriculum at limited scope. Experiential distillation explicitly outside standard curriculum — requires specialized instruction.
variations_known: Material distillation (inert source, stable, commercially practiced)
Experiential distillation (living source, controversial, requires consent)
Residue distillation (extracting from historical resonance residue — no living source required, extremely rare, produces weaker but more historically interesting effects)
techniques.visual_effect_tags: thin thread of golden light drawing upward from source material or person into practitioner's cupped hands, source material dimming slightly as essence lifts free, concentrate forming as glowing liquid drop between palms, warm amber concentration point with everything else slightly dimmed, finished vial containing swirling golden-amber light

techniques.name: Resonance Deep Read
techniques.description: Perceiving the complete material and resonance composition of a substance or object — not just what happened to it (Echo Sight) but what it is made of at every structural level, including accumulated Resonance residue layers, embedded inscriptions, True Smith bindings, and Founding Age material signatures. Requires Resonance Alchemy training rather than standard Resonance Arts — the perception is oriented toward material structure rather than event history. Practitioners use this as a diagnostic tool before any complex alchemical work. A Deep Read on a True Smithed object reveals the maker's signature — a fact that most True Smiths do not know.
techniques.game_mechanic_concept: Diagnostic ability: reveals full material composition, structural resonance properties, historical modification layers, and any embedded alchemy or True Smithing modifications. Passive application when examining objects. High skill level reveals curse-binding modifications, maker signatures on True Smithed items, and Founding Age construction techniques. Overexposure to high-complexity objects causes temporary perceptual overload.
techniques.cost: Low for simple objects. Scaling for objects with complex histories or dense resonance modification layers. High-residue objects risk perceptual overwhelm.
techniques.how_to_learn: Specialized Resonance Alchemy development — not in Guild curriculum. Requires alchemy training combined with Resonance perception development.
variations_known: Surface read (quick, reveals top layer only — available to any trained alchemist)
Deep read (full structural analysis — requires Resonance Alchemy specialization)
Historical read (traces modification history layer by layer, from oldest to most recent — rare, very slow, very revealing)
techniques.visual_effect_tags: practitioner's eyes shifting to deep amber alchemical sight, faint chemical structural lines overlaid on object surface, resonance residue visible as colored depth strata, practitioner's expression focused and still, quiet diagnostic intensity

techniques.name: True Forge Bind
techniques.description: The core act of True Smithing — integrating a specific resonance property into metal during the forging process. The smith maintains conscious Resonance awareness through the material while physically working it, holds the intended property as a lived experience (not an abstract concept), and channels that state into the metal through the sustained connection. The property becomes part of the material's grain structure — not deposited on it, not enchanted after the fact. It is the thing itself. Cannot be removed without destroying the object. The property in the finished object reflects the clarity and intensity of what the smith experienced while binding it.
techniques.game_mechanic_concept: Core smithing enchantment: during active forging, choose one property to permanently bind into the object being crafted. Available base properties: edge retention, structural durability, resonance resistance (minor), signature lock (see tech_011), material sensing. Property strength scales with smith's skill and mental state quality during binding. Multi-property objects require Long Work (tech_013). Bound properties cannot be removed post-fabrication.
techniques.cost: High. Sustained Resonance attention through the full physical working period — distraction or exhaustion during binding reduces property quality. Physically and mentally exhausting for complex properties.
techniques.how_to_learn: True Smithing training — either Guild licensed curriculum (covers basic to intermediate binding) or traditional lineage instruction (which predates Guild organization). The technique itself requires both Open Path access and smithing skill.
variations_known: Basic bind (single property, single session, moderate quality ceiling)
Precision bind (single property, multiple focused sessions, higher quality)
Long Work bind (multi-property, multi-day, highest quality ceiling — see tech_013)
techniques.visual_effect_tags: intense forge fire with resonance shimmer in the flames, hammer striking metal with light-trails following each blow, metal surface showing shifting inner glow as grain structure integrates the binding, smith's eyes reflecting metal light rather than forge fire, visible resonance currents in heated metal

techniques.name: Signature Lock Forging
techniques.description: Binding a specific person's Open Path signature into an object as a functional requirement — the object's True Smithed properties activate only for the person whose signature was matched at forging. Requires the bonded individual present during the final sealing phase, their resonance in direct contact with the metal as the binding closes. The signature becomes part of the object's material identity. A signature-locked blade is not simply reluctant in the hands of another — it is genuinely different: the bonded properties are inert, the edge still present but the resonance integration dormant. For locks, only the bonded individual can open them — the mechanism is not locked to anyone else, it simply does not function as a lock for the bonded person, as if the locking condition was never met.
techniques.game_mechanic_concept: Crafting ability: produce an object with properties active only for one specific individual. Non-bonded users receive no benefit from bound properties (item still functions as its base material, but True Smithed enhancement is absent). For locks: impassable to all non-bonded individuals. Bonding is permanent and non-transferable. Requires bonded individual present at final phase of crafting.
techniques.cost: High. Both smith and bonded subject expend Resonance energy at the sealing. Bonded individual experiences brief fatigue. If the bonded individual's resonance is not clear and stable at the sealing moment, the binding may seat incorrectly — producing a signature match that is imprecise and therefore less reliable.
techniques.how_to_learn: Advanced True Smithing — Guild curriculum covers basic version. Traditional lineage instruction covers full technique.
variations_known: Single-signature lock (standard — one person only)
Dual-signature lock (extremely rare — two people, both must be present at forging — requires advanced skill and significantly higher Resonance cost)
Time-conditional lock (signature match required AND specific environmental conditions — the most complex lock work currently practiced)
techniques.visual_effect_tags: bonded person's hand flat on cooling metal, smith's hand over theirs completing the seal, gold-amber resonance light flowing from subject's palm into metal, unique pattern impression forming on metal surface, brief intense resonance flare that subsides to persistent warm glow, both participants showing visible effort

techniques.name: Grain Weave
techniques.description: The foundation of True Smithing — working metal while maintaining continuous Resonance awareness through the material, feeling the grain structure through Open Path perception and guiding the physical work to align it optimally. A blade worked with Grain Weave has material consistency that conventional metallurgy cannot replicate: the grain runs without interruption through the length, the stress distribution is perfect, the edge geometry is precise in ways that appear almost mathematical. Standard smithing is external — you work the surface and infer the interior. Grain Weave is internal — you feel the interior and work from it. Brix's family calls this 'keeping company with the metal.' The Guild curriculum calls it 'resonance-assisted quality monitoring.' Both descriptions are accurate and both miss the point.
techniques.game_mechanic_concept: Passive technique: active throughout all True Smithing work. Improves finished object's base physical properties (durability, edge geometry, structural integrity, weight distribution) significantly above standard smithing equivalents. Requires sustained Resonance attention — distraction or fatigue causes quality degradation. Available to any practitioner with Open Path access and smithing skill, even without formal True Smithing training.
techniques.cost: Moderate Resonance energy sustained over the full working period. Available to lower-tier practitioners than full True Forge Bind.
techniques.how_to_learn: Teachable without formal curriculum — any practitioner with Open Path awareness who learns smithing can develop this intuitively. It is the most commonly practiced True Smithing technique and the one most likely to be independently discovered.
variations_known: Passive grain reading (sensing without directing — diagnostic use)
Active grain direction (guiding grain structure during working — standard Grain Weave)
Deep structure influence (influencing material properties at the crystalline level — requires Tier 3 or higher, produces extraordinary results in appropriate materials)
techniques.visual_effect_tags: smith's hands showing faint amber resonance glow where they contact metal, hammer work producing light-trails at impact points, heated metal showing internal structural patterns briefly visible during working, smith's eyes slightly unfocused as attention is divided between physical and resonance perception, work environment quiet and focused

techniques.name: The Long Work
techniques.first_demonstrated_by: Brix (anticipated — she practices this without the formal name)
techniques.description: Multi-day True Smithing technique — maintaining a continuous Resonance channel to an object across the full duration of the project, sleeping near it, returning across multiple sessions, building the property integration layer by layer over days or weeks. Each day of sustained work adds depth to the integration — the property becomes more fully part of the material, stronger, more stable, more precise. Objects produced through the Long Work have a character to them that single-session work cannot produce: the resonance is deeper, the properties more consistent, the overall quality carrying something that experienced practitioners call 'intention fully realized.' Brix's grandfather called it 'sitting time.' It is also the only way to produce multi-property objects — each property layer must be built separately, with adequate time between layers for the previous layer to fully integrate.
techniques.game_mechanic_concept: Extended crafting mode: minimum 7 in-story days. Each additional day of sustained work adds one integration layer (maximum 5). Each layer multiplies property strength and stability. Single-session smithing caps at 1 layer. Long Work can achieve up to 5 layers with mastery. Multi-property objects require one full Long Work session per property. The Resonance channel must not be broken during the project — significant physical incapacitation, extended separation, or major emotional disruption severs the channel and the work must restart.
techniques.cost: Daily Resonance investment across the full project period. Smith cannot perform other significant resonance work during active Long Work. The ongoing channel creates a kind of relationship between smith and object — practitioners often describe Long Work projects as emotionally significant in a way single-session work is not.
techniques.how_to_learn: Traditional smithing lineages teach this intuitively — 'the sitting time.' Guild curriculum does not formally cover Long Work above 3-day projects. Full 7-day-plus Long Work is either traditional lineage knowledge or very advanced Guild-licensed training.
variations_known: Standard Long Work (7-14 days, 3-4 layers, most common advanced use)
Deep Long Work (21+ days, 5 layers, very rare — the two objects Brix has produced this way are the best True Smithed items currently in use in Caelum City without anyone knowing it)
Collaborative Long Work (theoretical — two smiths maintaining the channel simultaneously to produce an object neither could produce alone; no confirmed modern examples)
techniques.visual_effect_tags: time-passage visual of smith returning to same partially-completed object across multiple days, each session showing the object's inner glow deepening in intensity and color richness, accumulated resonance depth visible as warm amber layers within the metal, finished object carrying visible internal luminance compared to single-session work, profound quiet surrounding the completed piece

meta.instruction: Add new entries whenever a technique is named, demonstrated, or meaningfully described in an episode. The game team will use this file directly.
